starting_equipment_options.desc: (a) a light crossbow and 20 bolts or (b) any simple weapon
(a) studded leather armor or (b) scale mail

1.name: Magical Tinkering
1.desc:
You’ve learned how to invest a spark of magic into mundane objects. To use this ability, you must have thieves’ tools or artisan’s tools in hand. You then touch a Tiny nonmagical object as an action and give it one of the following magical properties of your choice:

- The object sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet.
- Whenever tapped by a creature, the object emits a recorded message that can be heard up to 10 feet away. You utter the message when you bestow this property on the object, and the recording can be no more than 6 seconds long.
- The object continuously emits your choice of an odor or a nonverbal sound (wind, waves, chirping, or the like). The chosen phenomenon is perceptible up to 10 feet away.
- A static visual effect appears on one of the object’s surfaces. This effect can be a picture, up to 25 words of text, lines and shapes, or a mixture of these elements, as you like.

The chosen property lasts indefinitely. As an action, you can touch the object and end the property early.

You can bestow magic on multiple objects, touching one object each time you use this feature, though a single object can only bear one property at a time. The maximum number of objects you can affect with this feature at one time is equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one object). If you try to exceed your maximum, the oldest property immediately ends, and then the new property applies.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.desc: You can cast artificer spells.

2.name: Infuse Item
2.desc: You've gained the ability to imbue mundane items with certain magical infusions, turning those objects into magic items.

3.name: Artificer Specialty
3.desc: you choose the type of specialist you are. Your choice grants you features at 5th level and again at 9th and 15th level.  You may choose from: Alchemist, Armorer, Artillerist, or Battle Smith.

3.name: The Right Tool for the Job
3.desc: You've learned how to produce exactly the tool you need: with thieves' tools or artisan's tools in hand, you can magically create one set of artisan's tools in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. This creation requires 1 hour of uninterrupted work, which can coincide with a short or long rest. Though the product of magic, the tools are nonmagical, and they vanish when you use this feature again.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

5.name: Artificer Specialist feature
5.desc: You gain an ability based on your chosen specialization.

6.name: Tool Expertise
6.desc: your proficiency bonus is now doubled for any ability check you make that uses your proficiency with a tool.

7.name: Flash of Genius
7.desc: You've gained the ability to come up with solutions under pressure. When you or another creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an ability check or a saving throw, you can use your reaction to add your Intelligence modifier to the roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

9.name: Artificer Specialist feature
9.desc: You gain an ability based on your chosen specialization.

10.name: Magic Item Adept
10.desc:
You've achieved a profound understanding of how to use and make magic items.

- You can attune to up to four magic items at once.
- If you craft a magic item with a rarity of common or uncommon, it takes you a quarter of the normal time, and it costs you half as much of the usual gold.

11.name: Spell-Storing Item
11.desc:
You can now store a spell in an object.

When you finish a long rest, you can touch one simple or martial weapon or one item that you can use as a spellcasting focus, and store a 1st- or 2nd-level artificer spell in it. The spell must have a casting time of 1 action and must require you to expend a spell slot.

- While holding the object, a creature can take an action to produce the spell’s effect from it.
- The spell uses your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.
- The object can be used a number of times equal to twice your Intelligence modifier (minimum of twice).

The item loses the stored spell when you finish a long rest.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

14.name: Magic Item Savant
14.desc:
Your skill with magic items deepens.

- You can attune to up to five magic items at once.
- You ignore all class, race, spell, and level requirements on attuning to or using a magic item.

15.name: Artificer Specialist feature
15.desc: You gain an ability based on your chosen specialization.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

18.name: Magic Item Master
18.desc: You can now attune to up to six magic items at once.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

20.name: Soul of Artifice
20.desc:
You have developed a mystical connection to your magic items.

- You gain a +1 bonus to all saving throws for each magic item you are currently attuned to.
- If you're reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can use your reaction to end one of your artificer infusions, causing you to drop to 1 hit point instead.

1.name: Rage
1.desc:
In battle, you fight with primal ferocity. On your turn, you can enter a rage as a bonus action.

While raging, you gain the following benefits if you aren't wearing heavy armor:

- You have advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.
- When you make a melee weapon attack using Strength, you gain a bonus to the damage roll (see Rage Damage in progression).
- You have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

If you are able to cast spells, you can't cast them or concentrate on them while raging.

Your rage lasts for 1 minute. It ends early if you are knocked unconscious or if your turn ends and you haven't attacked a hostile creature since your last turn or taken damage since then. You can also end your rage on your turn as a bonus action.

Once you have raged the number of times shown for your barbarian level, you must finish a long rest before you can rage again.

1.name: Unarmored Defense
1.desc: While you are not wearing any armor, your armor class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit.

2.name: Reckless Attack
2.desc:
When you make your first attack on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly.

- You gain advantage on melee weapon attack rolls using Strength during this turn.
- Attack rolls against you have advantage until your next turn.

2.name: Danger Sense
2.desc:
You have advantage on Dexterity saving throws against effects that you can see, such as traps and spells.

To gain this benefit, you can't be blinded, deafened, or incapacitated.

3.name: Primal Path
3.desc: You choose a path that shapes the nature of your rage. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th levels.

3.name: Primal Knowledge (Optional)
3.desc: When you reach 3rd level and again at 10th level, you gain proficiency in one skill of your choice from the list of skills available to barbarians at 1st level.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.desc: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

5.name: Fast Movement
5.desc: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing heavy armor.

6.name: Primal Path feature
6.desc: You gain an ability based on your chosen Primal Path.

7.name: Feral Instinct
7.desc:
You have advantage on initiative rolls.

Additionally, if you are surprised at the beginning of combat and aren't incapacitated, you can act normally on your first turn, but only if you enter your rage before doing anything else on that turn.

7.name: Instinctive Pounce (Optional)
7.desc: As part of the bonus action you take to enter your rage, you can move up to half your speed.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

9.name: Brutal Critical
9.desc:
You can roll one additional weapon damage die when determining the extra damage for a critical hit with a melee attack.

This increases to two additional dice at 13th level and three additional dice at 17th level.

10.name: Primal Path feature
10.desc: You gain an ability based on your chosen Primal Path.

10.name: Primal Knowledge (Optional)
10.desc: You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice from the list of skills available to barbarians at 1st level.

11.name: Relentless Rage
11.desc:
If you drop to 0 hit points while you're raging and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, you drop to 1 hit point instead.

Each time you use this feature after the first, the DC increases by 5. When you finish a short or long rest, the DC resets to 10.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

13.name: Brutal Critical
13.desc: Your Brutal Critical improves: you roll two additional weapon damage dice on a melee critical hit.

14.name: Primal Path feature
14.desc: You gain an ability based on your chosen Primal Path.

15.name: Persistent Rage
15.desc: Your rage is so fierce that it ends early only if you fall unconscious or if you choose to end it.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

17.name: Brutal Critical
17.desc: Your Brutal Critical improves: you roll three additional weapon damage dice on a melee critical hit.

18.name: Indomitable Might
18.desc: If your total for a Strength check is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

20.name: Primal Champion
20.desc: Your Strength and Constitution scores increase by 4. Your maximum for those scores is now 24.

actions.name: Rage
actions.desc:
Enter a rage as a bonus action on your turn.

While raging (and not wearing heavy armor):
- Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.
- Bonus damage on melee weapon attacks using Strength.
- Resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

Rage lasts 1 minute and can end early under the normal Rage rules.

actions.name: End Rage
actions.desc: You can end your rage on your turn as a bonus action.

actions.name: Reckless Attack
actions.desc:
When you make your first attack on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly.

- You gain advantage on melee weapon attack rolls using Strength during this turn.
- Attack rolls against you have advantage until your next turn.

actions.name: Relentless Rage
actions.desc:
Trigger: You drop to 0 hit points while raging and don't die outright.

Make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a success, you drop to 1 hit point instead.

- Each use after the first increases the DC by 5.
- The DC resets to 10 when you finish a short or long rest.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.desc: You can cast bard spells.

1.name: Bardic Inspiration
1.desc: As a bonus action, choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet who can hear you. That creature gains a Bardic Inspiration die (d6). Within 10 minutes it can add the die to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw. Uses equal to your CHA modifier (min 1) per long rest. Die increases to d8 at 5th, d10 at 10th, d12 at 15th.

2.name: Jack of All Trades
2.desc: Add half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) to any ability check you make that doesn't already include your proficiency bonus.

2.name: Song of Rest
2.desc: During a short rest, if a creature regains hit points by spending Hit Dice and can hear your performance, it regains extra hit points (1d6). This die improves at 9th (1d8), 13th (1d10), and 17th (1d12).

2.name: Magical Inspiration (Optional)
2.desc: If a creature has your Bardic Inspiration die and casts a spell that restores hit points or deals damage, it can roll that die and add it to one target's healing or damage; the inspiration die is then lost.

3.name: Bard College
3.desc: Choose a bard college; your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th and 14th level.

3.name: Expertise
3.desc: Choose two skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for ability checks using either. At 10th level, choose two more skills.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

4.name: Bardic Versatility (Optional)
4.desc: Whenever you gain Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one Expertise skill (with another you have that isn't benefiting from Expertise) and/or replace one bard cantrip you know with another from the bard spell list.

5.name: Bardic Inspiration Improvement
5.desc: Your Bardic Inspiration die becomes a d8.

5.name: Font of Inspiration
5.desc: You regain all expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a short or long rest.

6.name: Countercharm
6.desc: As an action, start a performance that lasts until the end of your next turn. During that time, you and friendly creatures within 30 feet who can hear you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened or charmed.

6.name: Bard College feature
6.desc: You gain an ability based on your chosen Bard College.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

8.name: Bardic Versatility (Optional)
8.desc: Whenever you gain Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one Expertise skill (with another you have that isn't benefiting from Expertise) and/or replace one bard cantrip you know with another from the bard spell list.

9.name: Song of Rest Improvement
9.desc: Your Song of Rest die becomes a d8.

10.name: Bardic Inspiration Improvement
10.desc: Your Bardic Inspiration die becomes a d10.

10.name: Expertise
10.desc: Choose two more skill proficiencies to gain Expertise.

10.name: Magical Secrets
10.desc: Choose two spells from any class (including this one). A chosen spell must be of a level you can cast or a cantrip. The chosen spells count as bard spells for you and are included in your spells known.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

12.name: Bardic Versatility (Optional)
12.desc: Whenever you gain Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one Expertise skill (with another you have that isn't benefiting from Expertise) and/or replace one bard cantrip you know with another from the bard spell list.

13.name: Song of Rest Improvement
13.desc: Your Song of Rest die becomes a d10.

14.name: Magical Secrets
14.desc: You learn two additional spells from any class (as per Magical Secrets).

14.name: Bard College feature
14.desc: You gain an ability based on your chosen Bard College.

15.name: Bardic Inspiration Improvement
15.desc: Your Bardic Inspiration die becomes a d12.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

16.name: Bardic Versatility (Optional)
16.desc: Whenever you gain Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one Expertise skill (with another you have that isn't benefiting from Expertise) and/or replace one bard cantrip you know with another from the bard spell list.

17.name: Song of Rest Improvement
17.desc: Your Song of Rest die becomes a d12.

18.name: Magical Secrets
18.desc: You learn two additional spells from any class (as per Magical Secrets).

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. You may instead choose to take a Feat.

19.name: Bardic Versatility (Optional)
19.desc: Whenever you gain Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one Expertise skill (with another you have that isn't benefiting from Expertise) and/or replace one bard cantrip you know with another from the bard spell list.

20.name: Superior Inspiration
20.desc: When you roll initiative and have no uses of Bardic Inspiration left, you regain one use.

actions.name: Bardic Inspiration
actions.desc: Choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet who can hear you; it gains one Bardic Inspiration die.

actions.name: Countercharm
actions.desc: Start a performance until end of your next turn; you and friendly creatures within 30 feet who can hear you have advantage on saves vs frightened or charmed.

starting_equipment_options.desc: (a) a mace or (b) a warhammer (if proficient)
(a) scale mail, (b) leather armor, or (c) chain mail (if proficient)
(a) a light crossbow and 20 bolts or (b) any simple weapon
(a) a priest's pack or (b) an explorer's pack

1.name: Spellcasting
1.desc: As a conduit for divine power, you can cast cleric spells. You know three cleric cantrips. You prepare cleric spells each day (Wisdom modifier + cleric level; minimum 1).

1.name: Divine Domain
1.desc: Choose a Divine Domain. You gain domain spells and other features at 1st level, plus additional ways to use Channel Divinity at 2nd level, and additional benefits at 6th, 8th, and 17th levels.

2.name: Channel Divinity
2.desc: You can channel divine energy to fuel magical effects. You start with Turn Undead and a Channel Divinity option granted by your domain. You can use Channel Divinity once between rests.

2.name: Channel Divinity: Turn Undead
2.desc: As an action, present your holy symbol and speak a prayer censuring the undead. Each undead within 30 feet that can see or hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw; on a failure, it is turned for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

2.name: Divine Domain feature
2.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Divine Domain.

2.name: Harness Divine Power (Optional)
2.desc: As a bonus action, expend a use of Channel Divinity to regain one expended spell slot (max level = half your proficiency bonus, rounded up). Uses per long rest scale with cleric level.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

4.name: Cantrip Versatility (Optional)
4.desc: Whenever you gain Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one cleric cantrip you learned with another from the cleric spell list.

5.name: Destroy Undead (CR 1/2)
5.desc: When an undead fails its saving throw against Turn Undead, it is instantly destroyed if its challenge rating is 1/2 or lower.

6.name: Channel Divinity Improvement
6.desc: You can use Channel Divinity twice between rests.

6.name: Divine Domain feature
6.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Divine Domain.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

8.name: Destroy Undead (CR 1)
8.desc: When an undead fails its saving throw against Turn Undead, it is instantly destroyed if its challenge rating is 1 or lower.

8.name: Divine Domain feature
8.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Divine Domain.

8.name: Cantrip Versatility (Optional)
8.desc: Whenever you gain Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one cleric cantrip you learned with another from the cleric spell list.

8.name: Blessed Strikes (Optional)
8.desc: Replaces the Divine Strike or Potent Spellcasting feature. When a creature takes damage from one of your cantrips or weapon attacks, you can also deal 1d8 radiant damage to that creature; once per turn.

10.name: Divine Intervention
10.desc: As an action, describe the assistance you seek and roll d100. If the roll is equal to or lower than your cleric level, your deity intervenes (DM determines effect). On a success, you can't use this feature again for 7 days; otherwise you can try again after a long rest.

11.name: Destroy Undead (CR 2)
11.desc: When an undead fails its saving throw against Turn Undead, it is instantly destroyed if its challenge rating is 2 or lower.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

12.name: Cantrip Versatility (Optional)
12.desc: Whenever you gain Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one cleric cantrip you learned with another from the cleric spell list.

14.name: Destroy Undead (CR 3)
14.desc: When an undead fails its saving throw against Turn Undead, it is instantly destroyed if its challenge rating is 3 or lower.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

16.name: Cantrip Versatility (Optional)
16.desc: Whenever you gain Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one cleric cantrip you learned with another from the cleric spell list.

17.name: Destroy Undead (CR 4)
17.desc: When an undead fails its saving throw against Turn Undead, it is instantly destroyed if its challenge rating is 4 or lower.

17.name: Divine Domain feature
17.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Divine Domain.

18.name: Channel Divinity Improvement
18.desc: You can use Channel Divinity three times between rests.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

19.name: Cantrip Versatility (Optional)
19.desc: Whenever you gain Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one cleric cantrip you learned with another from the cleric spell list.

20.name: Divine Intervention Improvement
20.desc: Your Divine Intervention succeeds automatically; no roll required.

actions.name: Channel Divinity: Turn Undead
actions.desc: Present your holy symbol; each undead within 30 feet that can see or hear you makes a WIS save or is turned for 1 minute (or until it takes damage).

actions.name: Harness Divine Power (Optional)
actions.desc: Expend Channel Divinity to regain one expended spell slot (max level = half your proficiency bonus, rounded up).

actions.name: Divine Intervention
actions.desc: Roll d100; if result ≤ cleric level, your deity intervenes (DM determines effect).

starting_equipment_options.desc: (a) a shield or (b) any simple weapon
(a) a scimitar or (b) any simple melee weapon

1.name: Spellcasting
1.desc: You can cast druid spells. You prepare druid spells each day equal to your Wisdom modifier + your druid level (minimum 1).

1.name: Druidic
1.desc: You know Druidic, the secret language of druids. You can speak the language and use it to leave hidden messages.

2.name: Wild Shape
2.desc: As an action, you can magically assume the shape of a beast you have seen before. You can use this feature twice per short or long rest. The maximum beast CR and movement restrictions improve with druid level.

2.name: Druid Circle
2.desc: Choose a Druid Circle, which grants features at 2nd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th level.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

4.name: Cantrip Versatility (Optional)
4.desc: Whenever you gain an Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one druid cantrip you know with another from the druid spell list.

6.name: Druid Circle feature
6.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Druid Circle.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

8.name: Wild Shape Improvement
8.desc: You can transform into beasts with a challenge rating as high as 1. You can now assume forms with a swimming speed.

8.name: Cantrip Versatility (Optional)
8.desc: Whenever you gain an Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one druid cantrip you know with another from the druid spell list.

10.name: Druid Circle feature
10.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Druid Circle.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

12.name: Cantrip Versatility (Optional)
12.desc: Whenever you gain an Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one druid cantrip you know with another from the druid spell list.

14.name: Druid Circle feature
14.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Druid Circle.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

16.name: Cantrip Versatility (Optional)
16.desc: Whenever you gain an Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one druid cantrip you know with another from the druid spell list.

18.name: Timeless Body
18.desc: The primal magic that you wield causes you to age more slowly. For every 10 years that pass, your body ages only 1 year.

18.name: Beast Spells
18.desc: You can cast many of your druid spells in any shape you assume using Wild Shape.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

19.name: Cantrip Versatility (Optional)
19.desc: Whenever you gain an Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one druid cantrip you know with another from the druid spell list.

20.name: Archdruid
20.desc: You can use Wild Shape an unlimited number of times, and can ignore verbal and somatic components of spells while in beast shape.

actions.name: Wild Shape
actions.desc: Magically assume the shape of a beast you have seen before.

1.name: Fighting Style
1.desc: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one Fighting Style option.

1.name: Second Wind
1.desc: On your turn, you can use a bonus action to regain hit points equal to 1d10 + your fighter level. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

2.name: Action Surge
2.desc:
Starting at 2nd level, you can push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment. On your turn, you can take one additional action on top of your regular action and a possible bonus action.

Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again. Starting at 17th level, you can use it twice before a rest, but only once on the same turn.

3.name: Martial Archetype
3.desc: Choose a Martial Archetype. Your archetype grants features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

4.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
4.desc: Whenever you reach a fighter level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to fighters. If you know any Battle Master maneuvers, you can also replace one maneuver you know with a different one.

5.name: Extra Attack (x1)
5.desc: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

6.name: Ability Score Improvement
6.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

6.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
6.desc: Whenever you reach a fighter level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to fighters. If you know any Battle Master maneuvers, you can also replace one maneuver you know with a different one.

7.name: Martial Archetype feature
7.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Martial Archetype.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

8.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
8.desc: Whenever you reach a fighter level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to fighters. If you know any Battle Master maneuvers, you can also replace one maneuver you know with a different one.

9.name: Indomitable (x1)
9.desc: You can reroll a saving throw that you fail. If you do so, you must use the new roll. Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

10.name: Martial Archetype feature
10.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Martial Archetype.

11.name: Extra Attack (x2)
11.desc: You can attack three times whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

12.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
12.desc: Whenever you reach a fighter level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to fighters. If you know any Battle Master maneuvers, you can also replace one maneuver you know with a different one.

13.name: Indomitable (x2)
13.desc: You can use Indomitable twice between long rests.

14.name: Ability Score Improvement
14.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

14.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
14.desc: Whenever you reach a fighter level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to fighters. If you know any Battle Master maneuvers, you can also replace one maneuver you know with a different one.

15.name: Martial Archetype feature
15.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Martial Archetype.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

16.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
16.desc: Whenever you reach a fighter level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to fighters. If you know any Battle Master maneuvers, you can also replace one maneuver you know with a different one.

17.name: Action Surge (x2)
17.desc: You can use Action Surge twice between rests (but only once on the same turn).

17.name: Indomitable (x3)
17.desc: You can use Indomitable three times between long rests.

18.name: Martial Archetype feature
18.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Martial Archetype.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

19.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
19.desc: Whenever you reach a fighter level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to fighters. If you know any Battle Master maneuvers, you can also replace one maneuver you know with a different one.

20.name: Extra Attack (x3)
20.desc: You can attack four times whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

actions.name: Second Wind
actions.desc: Regain hit points equal to 1d10 + your fighter level.

actions.name: Action Surge
actions.desc: On your turn, take one additional action.

actions.name: Indomitable
actions.desc: Reroll a failed saving throw; you must use the new roll.

1.name: Unarmored Defense
1.desc: While you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier.

1.name: Martial Arts
1.desc: While you are unarmed or wielding only monk weapons, and you aren't wearing armor or wielding a shield: you can use Dexterity instead of Strength for attacks/damage with unarmed strikes and monk weapons; you can use the Martial Arts die in place of normal damage for your unarmed strikes/monk weapons; and when you use the Attack action with an unarmed strike or monk weapon, you can make one unarmed strike as a bonus action.

2.name: Ki
2.desc: You have ki points equal to your monk level (starting at 2). You regain all spent ki when you finish a short or long rest, and must meditate at least 30 minutes. Ki save DC = 8 + proficiency bonus + Wisdom modifier. You learn: Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, Step of the Wind.

2.name: Unarmored Movement
2.desc: Your speed increases while you are not wearing armor or wielding a shield. The bonus increases with monk level. At 9th level, you can move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the move.

2.name: Dedicated Weapon (Optional)
2.desc: Whenever you finish a short or long rest, you can touch one weapon and count it as a monk weapon until you use this feature again. The weapon must be a simple or martial weapon you are proficient with, and it must lack the heavy and special properties.

3.name: Monastic Tradition
3.desc: Choose a Monastic Tradition. You gain tradition features at 3rd level and again at 6th, 11th, and 17th level.

3.name: Deflect Missiles
3.desc: When you are hit by a ranged weapon attack, you can use your reaction to reduce the damage by 1d10 + your Dexterity modifier + your monk level. If you reduce the damage to 0, you can catch it (if small enough and you have a free hand). If you catch it, you can spend 1 ki point to make a ranged attack with it (20/60) as part of the same reaction; you are proficient, and it counts as a monk weapon.

3.name: Ki-Fueled Attack (Optional)
3.desc: If you spend 1 ki point or more as part of your action on your turn, you can make one attack with an unarmed strike or a monk weapon as a bonus action before the end of the turn.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

4.name: Slow Fall
4.desc: When you fall, you can use your reaction to reduce any falling damage you take by an amount equal to five times your monk level.

4.name: Quickened Healing (Optional)
4.desc: As an action, you can spend 2 ki points and roll a Martial Arts die. You regain hit points equal to the roll + your proficiency bonus.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.desc: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

5.name: Stunning Strike
5.desc: When you hit another creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 1 ki point to attempt a stunning strike. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be stunned until the end of your next turn.

5.name: Focused Aim (Optional)
5.desc: When you miss with an attack roll, you can spend 1 to 3 ki points to increase your attack roll by 2 for each ki point spent, potentially turning the miss into a hit.

6.name: Ki-Empowered Strikes
6.desc: Your unarmed strikes count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

6.name: Monastic Tradition feature
6.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Monastic Tradition.

7.name: Evasion
7.desc: When subjected to an effect that allows a Dexterity saving throw for half damage, you take no damage on a success and only half damage on a failure.

7.name: Stillness of Mind
7.desc: You can use your action to end one effect on yourself that is causing you to be charmed or frightened.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

9.name: Unarmored Movement Improvement
9.desc: You can move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the move.

10.name: Purity of Body
10.desc: You are immune to disease and poison.

11.name: Monastic Tradition feature
11.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Monastic Tradition.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

13.name: Tongue of the Sun and Moon
13.desc: You understand all spoken languages, and any creature that can understand a language can understand what you say.

14.name: Diamond Soul
14.desc: You have proficiency in all saving throws. Additionally, when you fail a saving throw, you can spend 1 ki point to reroll it and take the second result.

15.name: Timeless Body
15.desc: You suffer none of the frailty of old age, and you can't be aged magically (though you can still die of old age). You no longer need food or water.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

17.name: Monastic Tradition feature
17.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Monastic Tradition.

18.name: Empty Body
18.desc: You can use your action to spend 4 ki points to become invisible for 1 minute; during that time you also have resistance to all damage but force. You can also spend 8 ki points to cast astral projection (no material components), without taking other creatures with you.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

20.name: Perfect Self
20.desc: When you roll initiative and have no ki points remaining, you regain 4 ki points.

actions.name: Martial Arts: Bonus Unarmed Strike
actions.desc: After you use the Attack action with an unarmed strike or monk weapon, you can make one unarmed strike as a bonus action.

actions.name: Flurry of Blows
actions.desc: Immediately after you take the Attack action, spend 1 ki point to make two unarmed strikes as a bonus action.

actions.name: Patient Defense
actions.desc: Spend 1 ki point to take the Dodge action as a bonus action.

actions.name: Step of the Wind
actions.desc: Spend 1 ki point to take the Disengage or Dash action as a bonus action; your jump distance is doubled for the turn.

actions.name: Ki-Fueled Attack (Optional)
actions.desc: If you spend 1 ki point or more as part of your action on your turn, you can make one attack with an unarmed strike or a monk weapon as a bonus action before the end of the turn.

actions.name: Deflect Missiles
actions.desc: When hit by a ranged weapon attack, reduce damage by 1d10 + DEX mod + monk level; if reduced to 0 you can catch it (free hand) and optionally spend 1 ki to throw it back as part of the reaction.

actions.name: Slow Fall
actions.desc: When you fall, reduce falling damage by 5 × your monk level.

actions.name: Stunning Strike
actions.desc: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 1 ki point; it makes a CON save or is stunned until the end of your next turn.

actions.name: Stillness of Mind
actions.desc: End one effect on yourself that is causing you to be charmed or frightened.

actions.name: Quickened Healing (Optional)
actions.desc: Spend 2 ki points; roll a Martial Arts die and regain that many hit points + your proficiency bonus.

actions.name: Empty Body
actions.desc: Spend 4 ki points to become invisible for 1 minute and gain resistance to all damage but force; or spend 8 ki points to cast astral projection (self only, no material components).

1.name: Divine Sense
1.desc: As an action, you can open your awareness to detect strong evil and powerful good. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any celestial, fiend, or undead within 60 feet that is not behind total cover, and you know the type. You also detect the presence of any place or object that has been consecrated or desecrated. Uses: 1 + CHA modifier per long rest.

1.name: Lay on Hands
1.desc: You have a pool of healing power that replenishes when you take a long rest. The pool equals 5 × your paladin level. As an action, you can touch a creature and restore hit points from the pool. Alternatively, you can expend 5 hit points from the pool to cure one disease or neutralize one poison affecting the creature.

2.name: Fighting Style
2.desc: Choose a Fighting Style option.

2.name: Spellcasting
2.desc: You can cast paladin spells. Spellcasting ability: Charisma. You prepare paladin spells each day.

2.name: Divine Smite
2.desc: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can expend one spell slot to deal radiant damage to the target, in addition to the weapon's damage. Damage is 2d8 for a 1st-level slot, +1d8 per slot level higher (max 5d8). Damage increases by 1d8 against undead or fiends.

3.name: Divine Health
3.desc: You are immune to disease.

3.name: Sacred Oath
3.desc: You swear the oath that binds you as a paladin forever. Choose a Sacred Oath; it grants features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 15th, and 20th levels. Your oath also grants oath spells and Channel Divinity options.

3.name: Channel Divinity
3.desc: Your Sacred Oath gives you Channel Divinity options. You can use your Channel Divinity once between short or long rests.

3.name: Harness Divine Power (Optional)
3.desc: As a bonus action, expend a use of Channel Divinity to regain one expended spell slot (max level = half your proficiency bonus, rounded up). Uses per long rest scale with paladin level.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

4.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
4.desc: Whenever you reach a paladin level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to paladins.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.desc: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

6.name: Aura of Protection
6.desc: Whenever you or a friendly creature within 10 feet of you must make a saving throw, the creature gains a bonus equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum +1). You must be conscious to grant this bonus. At 18th level, the range increases to 30 feet.

7.name: Sacred Oath feature
7.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Sacred Oath.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

8.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
8.desc: Whenever you reach a paladin level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to paladins.

10.name: Aura of Courage
10.desc: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you can't be frightened while you are conscious. At 18th level, the range increases to 30 feet.

11.name: Improved Divine Smite
11.desc: Whenever you hit a creature with a melee weapon, the creature takes an extra 1d8 radiant damage.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

12.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
12.desc: Whenever you reach a paladin level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to paladins.

14.name: Cleansing Touch
14.desc: As an action, you can end one spell on yourself or on one willing creature that you touch. Uses: Charisma modifier (minimum 1) per long rest.

15.name: Sacred Oath feature
15.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Sacred Oath.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

16.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
16.desc: Whenever you reach a paladin level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to paladins.

18.name: Aura Improvements
18.desc: The range of your Aura of Protection and Aura of Courage increases to 30 feet.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

19.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
19.desc: Whenever you reach a paladin level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to paladins.

20.name: Sacred Oath feature
20.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Sacred Oath.

actions.name: Divine Sense
actions.desc: Detect celestials, fiends, undead within 60 feet (not behind total cover) until end of your next turn; also detect consecrated/desecrated objects/places.

actions.name: Lay on Hands
actions.desc: Heal a creature using your pool (5 × paladin level), or spend 5 points to cure a disease or neutralize one poison.

actions.name: Divine Smite
actions.desc: When you hit with a melee weapon attack, expend a spell slot to deal radiant damage (2d8 +1d8/slot level above 1st, max 5d8; +1d8 vs undead/fiends).

actions.name: Harness Divine Power (Optional)
actions.desc: Expend Channel Divinity to regain one expended spell slot (max level = half your proficiency bonus, rounded up).

actions.name: Cleansing Touch
actions.desc: End one spell on yourself or a willing creature you touch (CHA modifier uses per long rest).

starting_equipment_options.desc: (a) two shortswords or (b) two simple melee weapons
(a) a dungeoneer's pack or (b) an explorer's pack

1.name: Favored Enemy
1.desc: Choose a type of favored enemy: aberrations, beasts, celestials, constructs, dragons, elementals, fey, fiends, giants, monstrosities, oozes, plants, or undead. Alternatively, choose two humanoid races. You have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track your favored enemies and on Intelligence checks to recall information about them. You also learn one language spoken by your favored enemies, if they speak one. You choose one additional favored enemy and language at 6th level and again at 14th level.

1.name: Natural Explorer
1.desc: Choose one type of favored terrain: arctic, coast, desert, forest, grassland, mountain, swamp, or the Underdark. When you make an Intelligence or Wisdom check related to your favored terrain, your proficiency bonus is doubled if using a skill you're proficient in. While traveling for an hour or more in your favored terrain, you gain travel benefits (difficult terrain doesn't slow group, can't become lost except magically, remain alert, etc.). You choose additional favored terrain types at 6th and 10th level.

1.name: Favored Foe (Optional)
1.desc: Replaces Favored Enemy. When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can mark it as your favored foe for 1 minute (or until you lose concentration, as if concentrating on a spell). The first time on each of your turns that you hit the favored foe and deal damage to it, you deal an extra 1d4 damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest. The extra damage increases to 1d6 at 6th level and 1d8 at 14th level.

1.name: Deft Explorer (Optional)
1.desc: Replaces Natural Explorer. You gain benefits as you advance: at 1st level choose Canny; at 6th level gain Roving; at 10th level gain Tireless.

2.name: Fighting Style
2.desc: Choose a Fighting Style option.

2.name: Spellcasting
2.desc: You can cast ranger spells. Spellcasting ability: Wisdom. You learn a number of ranger spells (Spells Known) as shown in the Ranger table.

3.name: Ranger Archetype
3.desc: Choose a Ranger Archetype. Your archetype grants features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 11th, and 15th level.

3.name: Primeval Awareness
3.desc: You can use your action and expend one ranger spell slot to focus your awareness on the region around you. For 1 minute per level of the spell slot expended, you can sense whether certain creature types are present within 1 mile (or within up to 6 miles in your favored terrain). This feature doesn't reveal location or number.

3.name: Primal Awareness (Optional)
3.desc: Replaces Primeval Awareness. You learn additional spells at certain ranger levels; they don't count against spells known. You can cast each of them once without expending a spell slot, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

4.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
4.desc: Whenever you reach a ranger level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to rangers.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.desc: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

7.name: Ranger Archetype feature
7.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Ranger Archetype.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

8.name: Land's Stride
8.desc: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You can also pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or similar hazards. You have advantage on saving throws against plants that are magically created or manipulated to impede movement.

8.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
8.desc: Whenever you reach a ranger level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to rangers.

10.name: Hide in Plain Sight
10.desc: You can spend 1 minute creating camouflage for yourself. You must have access to fresh mud, dirt, plants, soot, or other natural materials. Once camouflaged, you can try to hide by pressing yourself against a solid surface at least as tall and wide as you are. You gain a +10 bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) checks as long as you remain there without moving or taking actions. Once you move or take an action or a reaction, you must camouflage again to gain the benefit.

10.name: Nature's Veil (Optional)
10.desc: Replaces Hide in Plain Sight. As a bonus action, you can magically become invisible, along with equipment you are wearing or carrying, until the start of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest.

11.name: Ranger Archetype feature
11.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Ranger Archetype.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

12.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
12.desc: Whenever you reach a ranger level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to rangers.

14.name: Vanish
14.desc: You can use the Hide action as a bonus action on your turn. Also, you can't be tracked by nonmagical means unless you choose to leave a trail.

15.name: Ranger Archetype feature
15.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Ranger Archetype.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

16.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
16.desc: Whenever you reach a ranger level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to rangers.

18.name: Feral Senses
18.desc: You gain preternatural senses that help you fight creatures you can't see. When you attack a creature you can't see, your inability to see it doesn't impose disadvantage on your attack rolls against it. You are also aware of the location of any invisible creature within 30 feet of you, provided the creature isn't hidden from you and you aren't blinded or deafened.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

19.name: Martial Versatility (Optional)
19.desc: Whenever you reach a ranger level that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace a Fighting Style you know with another available to rangers.

20.name: Foe Slayer
20.desc: Once on each of your turns, you can add your Wisdom modifier to the attack roll or the damage roll of an attack you make against one of your favored enemies.

actions.name: Favored Foe (Optional)
actions.desc: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, mark it as your favored foe for 1 minute (concentration). First hit each turn deals +1d4 (scales to +1d6 at 6, +1d8 at 14). Uses = PB per long rest.

actions.name: Nature's Veil (Optional)
actions.desc: Become invisible (with your equipment) until the start of your next turn. Uses = PB per long rest.

actions.name: Vanish
actions.desc: You can take the Hide action as a bonus action on your turn.

1.name: Expertise
1.desc: Choose two of your skill proficiencies, or one of your skill proficiencies and your proficiency with thieves' tools. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.

1.name: Sneak Attack
1.desc: Once per turn, you can deal extra damage to one creature you hit with an attack if you have advantage on the attack roll. The attack must use a finesse or a ranged weapon. You don't need advantage if another enemy of the target is within 5 feet of it, that enemy isn't incapacitated, and you don't have disadvantage on the attack roll. The extra damage increases by level.

1.name: Thieves' Cant
1.desc: You know thieves' cant, a secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code. Only another creature that knows thieves' cant understands such messages. It takes four times longer to convey such a message than to speak the same idea plainly. You also understand a set of secret signs and symbols used to convey short, simple messages.

2.name: Cunning Action
2.desc: You can take a bonus action on each of your turns in combat. This action can be used only to take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

3.name: Roguish Archetype
3.desc: Choose a Roguish Archetype. Your archetype choice grants you features at 3rd level and then again at 9th, 13th, and 17th level.

3.name: Steady Aim (Optional)
3.desc: As a bonus action, you give yourself advantage on your next attack roll on the current turn. You can use this bonus action only if you haven't moved during this turn, and after you use it, your speed is 0 until the end of the current turn.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

5.name: Uncanny Dodge
5.desc: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.

6.name: Expertise
6.desc: Choose two more of your proficiencies (in skills or with thieves' tools). Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.

7.name: Evasion
7.desc: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed, and only half damage if you fail.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

9.name: Roguish Archetype feature
9.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Roguish Archetype.

10.name: Ability Score Improvement
10.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

11.name: Reliable Talent
11.desc: Whenever you make an ability check that lets you add your proficiency bonus, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

13.name: Roguish Archetype feature
13.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Roguish Archetype.

14.name: Blindsense
14.desc: If you are able to hear, you are aware of the location of any hidden or invisible creature within 10 feet of you.

15.name: Slippery Mind
15.desc: You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

17.name: Roguish Archetype feature
17.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Roguish Archetype.

18.name: Elusive
18.desc: No attack roll has advantage against you while you aren't incapacitated.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

20.name: Stroke of Luck
20.desc: If your attack misses a target within range, you can turn the miss into a hit. Alternatively, if you fail an ability check, you can treat the d20 roll as a 20. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

actions.name: Cunning Action
actions.desc: Take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action as a bonus action.

actions.name: Steady Aim (Optional)
actions.desc: Give yourself advantage on your next attack roll this turn, only if you haven't moved; your speed becomes 0 until end of turn.

actions.name: Uncanny Dodge
actions.desc: When an attacker you can see hits you with an attack, halve the attack's damage against you.

actions.name: Stroke of Luck
actions.desc: Turn a miss into a hit, or treat a failed ability check's d20 as a 20. Recharges on a short or long rest.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.desc: You can cast sorcerer spells. Spellcasting ability: Charisma. You know sorcerer cantrips and a limited number of sorcerer spells (Spells Known) as shown in the Sorcerer table; when you gain a level, you can replace one sorcerer spell you know with another from the sorcerer spell list (must be a level you can cast).

1.name: Sorcerous Origin
1.desc: Choose a sorcerous origin, which grants features at 1st level and again at 6th, 14th, and 18th level.

2.name: Font of Magic
2.desc: You have sorcery points equal to your sorcerer level (starting at 2), regained on a long rest. You can use Flexible Casting to convert spell slots into sorcery points and create spell slots using sorcery points.

3.name: Metamagic
3.desc: You gain Metamagic options that let you modify spells you cast by spending sorcery points. You learn two options at 3rd level, one more at 10th level, and one more at 17th level.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

4.name: Sorcerous Versatility (Optional)
4.desc: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one Metamagic option you know with another, or replace one sorcerer cantrip you know with a different sorcerer cantrip.

5.name: Magical Guidance (Optional)
5.desc: When you fail an ability check, you can spend 1 sorcery point to reroll the d20, and you must use the new roll.

6.name: Sorcerous Origin feature
6.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Sorcerous Origin.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

8.name: Sorcerous Versatility (Optional)
8.desc: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one Metamagic option you know with another, or replace one sorcerer cantrip you know with a different sorcerer cantrip.

10.name: Metamagic
10.desc: You learn one additional Metamagic option.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

12.name: Sorcerous Versatility (Optional)
12.desc: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one Metamagic option you know with another, or replace one sorcerer cantrip you know with a different sorcerer cantrip.

14.name: Sorcerous Origin feature
14.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Sorcerous Origin.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

16.name: Sorcerous Versatility (Optional)
16.desc: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one Metamagic option you know with another, or replace one sorcerer cantrip you know with a different sorcerer cantrip.

17.name: Metamagic
17.desc: You learn one additional Metamagic option.

18.name: Sorcerous Origin feature
18.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Sorcerous Origin.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

19.name: Sorcerous Versatility (Optional)
19.desc: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one Metamagic option you know with another, or replace one sorcerer cantrip you know with a different sorcerer cantrip.

20.name: Sorcerous Restoration
20.desc: You regain 4 expended sorcery points whenever you finish a short rest.

actions.name: Flexible Casting: Create Spell Slot
actions.desc: Convert sorcery points into one spell slot (up to 5th level). The created slot vanishes when you finish a long rest.

actions.name: Flexible Casting: Convert Spell Slot
actions.desc: Expend a spell slot to gain sorcery points equal to the slot's level (no action required).

actions.name: Magical Guidance (Optional)
actions.desc: When you fail an ability check, spend 1 sorcery point to reroll the d20; you must use the new roll.

1.name: Otherworldly Patron
1.desc: Choose an Otherworldly Patron, which grants features at 1st level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th level.

1.name: Pact Magic
1.desc: You can cast warlock spells. Spellcasting ability: Charisma. You know a limited number of spells (Spells Known) and warlock cantrips as shown in the Warlock table. Your spell slots are special: they refresh on a short or long rest, and all warlock spells you cast using Pact Magic are cast at the slot level shown for your warlock level.

2.name: Eldritch Invocations
2.desc: You gain eldritch invocations of your choice. You gain more invocations as you gain warlock levels. An invocation can have prerequisites.

3.name: Pact Boon
3.desc: Your otherworldly patron bestows a gift upon you for your loyal service. Choose one Pact Boon option.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

4.name: Eldritch Versatility (Optional)
4.desc: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one warlock cantrip you know with another from the warlock spell list, or replace your Pact Boon choice with another Pact Boon option.

6.name: Otherworldly Patron feature
6.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Otherworldly Patron.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

8.name: Eldritch Versatility (Optional)
8.desc: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one warlock cantrip you know with another from the warlock spell list, or replace your Pact Boon choice with another Pact Boon option.

10.name: Otherworldly Patron feature
10.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Otherworldly Patron.

11.name: Mystic Arcanum (6th level)
11.desc: Your patron bestows upon you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one 6th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum. You can cast it once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

12.name: Eldritch Versatility (Optional)
12.desc: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one warlock cantrip you know with another from the warlock spell list, or replace your Pact Boon choice with another Pact Boon option.

13.name: Mystic Arcanum (7th level)
13.desc: Choose one 7th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum. You can cast it once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

14.name: Otherworldly Patron feature
14.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Otherworldly Patron.

15.name: Mystic Arcanum (8th level)
15.desc: Choose one 8th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum. You can cast it once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

16.name: Eldritch Versatility (Optional)
16.desc: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one warlock cantrip you know with another from the warlock spell list, or replace your Pact Boon choice with another Pact Boon option.

17.name: Mystic Arcanum (9th level)
17.desc: Choose one 9th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum. You can cast it once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

19.name: Eldritch Versatility (Optional)
19.desc: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants Ability Score Improvement, you can replace one warlock cantrip you know with another from the warlock spell list, or replace your Pact Boon choice with another Pact Boon option.

20.name: Eldritch Master
20.desc: You can draw on your inner reserve of mystical power to regain all of your expended Pact Magic spell slots. You can spend 1 minute entreating your patron for aid to regain them. Once you regain slots with this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

actions.name: Eldritch Master
actions.desc: Spend 1 minute to regain all expended Pact Magic spell slots (once per long rest).

1.name: Spellcasting
1.desc: You can cast wizard spells. Spellcasting ability: Intelligence. You have a spellbook containing six 1st-level wizard spells of your choice. You prepare wizard spells each day based on your Intelligence modifier + wizard level.

1.name: Arcane Recovery
1.desc: Once per day when you finish a short rest, you can recover expended spell slots. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to or less than half your wizard level (rounded up), and none can be 6th level or higher.

2.name: Arcane Tradition
2.desc: Choose an Arcane Tradition, which grants features at 2nd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th level.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

6.name: Arcane Tradition feature
6.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Arcane Tradition.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

10.name: Arcane Tradition feature
10.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Arcane Tradition.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

14.name: Arcane Tradition feature
14.desc: You gain a feature from your chosen Arcane Tradition.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

18.name: Spell Mastery
18.desc: Choose a 1st-level wizard spell and a 2nd-level wizard spell that are in your spellbook. You can cast those spells at their lowest level without expending a spell slot, provided you have them prepared.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 (max 20). You may instead take a Feat.

20.name: Signature Spells
20.desc: Choose two 3rd-level wizard spells in your spellbook as your signature spells. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each of them once at 3rd level without expending a spell slot; you regain the ability to do so when you finish a short or long rest.

actions.name: Arcane Recovery
actions.desc: Once per day after a short rest, recover spell slots with a combined level equal to or less than half your wizard level (rounded up), none of which can be 6th level or higher.
